Украинский канал Exilenova+ опубликовал видео, которые, как утверждается, сняли очевидцы пожара. Российский телеграм-канал Astra верифицировал эти ролики. Местные власти сообщали, что ночью российские ПВО отразили налет украинских беспилотников под Невинномысском. Однако, по словам губернатора Владимира Владимирова, пострадавших и разрушений на земле в результате атаки нет. За время полномасштабной войны это как минимум шестая атака на предприятие, продукция которого среди прочего используют при создании артиллерийских снарядов. @meduzalive

description: Что говорят в Рязани об атаке украинских дронов, которые попали в НПЗ и два жилых дома В ночь на 15 мая ВСУ нанесли массированный удар по Рязани. Атака произошла на следующий день после того, как президент Украины Владимир Зеленский поручил военным дать ответ на российские обстрелы. В результате одного из таких ударов РФ по Киеву погибли 24 человека. В Рязани дроны попали в нефтеперерабатывающий завод, один из крупнейших в России, и в две многоэтажки — дом на улице Новоселов и в ЖК «Триколор». В результате погибли четыре человека, в том числе ребенок, еще 12 человек пострадали. Вот что рассказала жительница Рязани, у подруги которой на улице Новоселов жили родные: Был сильный взрыв, говорят, удар пришелся на третий и четвертый этажи. Обвалился потолок. Сама подруга живет отдельно с дочерью. В квартире на Новоселов были ее мать, брат и бабушка. Брат и бабушка погибли. Тела вытаскивали из-под обломков, подруга ездила на опознание. К Дональду Трампу присоединились основатель SpaceX и гендиректор Tesla Илон Маск, глава Apple Тим Кук, руководители Meta, Goldman Sachs, Boeing, Visa и других компаний. Однако вряд ли можно сказать, что их присутствие сильно на что-то повлияло. Более того, после двух дней, которые Трамп провел в Пекине, в принципе мало понятно, зачем именно он приезжал. А вот Си Цзиньпин четко знал, какого результата хочет добиться от этой встречи. Именно так считает китаист и директор Берлинского центра Карнеги по изучению России и Евразии Александр Габуев. В тексте для проекта Carnegie Politika он анализирует встречу двух лидеров, а также рассказывает, чего дальше ожидать от отношений Китая и США. «Медуза» публикует этот текст целиком. description: Павел Павликовский снял свой лучший фильм — о странной поездке Томаса и Эрики Манн в ГДР и ФРГ Первый очевидный шедевр из Канн-2026: долгожданная драма Павликовского (оскаровского и каннского лауреата) «Фатерлянд», которая завершает его трилогию о послевоенной Европе. В 1949 году обе Германии — ФРГ и ГДР — празднуют 200 лет со дня рождения национального поэта Гете. Выступить на торжествах приглашают Томаса Манна — «совесть нации», едва ли не самого знаменитого немца, призывавшего к борьбе с Гитлером. Манн и его дочь Эрика едут домой впервые за 16 лет отсутствия — и находят там мало поводов для радости: да, нацистский режим пал, но лжи в стране как будто не стало меньше. В «Фатерлянд» мощнейший каст. Томаса Манна играет ветеран европейского кино Ханнс Цишлер. Эрику — звезда фестивалей, а теперь и блокбастеров («Проект „Аве Мария”») Сандра Хюллер. Еще одного члена семейства Манн, Клауса — Аугуст Диль, Воланд из «Мастера и Маргариты» Локшина. Вот что пишет о картине Антон Долин: «Фатерлянд» — невероятный фильм. Его концентрированная сдержанная сила не нуждается ни в большом бюджете, ни в постановочном размахе: даже хронометраж меньше полутора часов. Он умно и точно написан, виртуозно сыгран и умопомрачительно снят. Это очень формалистичное кино, но чуть ли не важнее формальной стороны — прицельная точность каждой сцены. Павликовский недаром начинал как документалист: он фиксирует исторические события предельно достоверно — и при этом умудряется описать именно тот мир, в котором мы живем сегодня. title: Telegram Post from meduzalive
ad_reason: Brand mention detected without enough advertising evidence.
description: «Пожалуйста, живи». Книга Ланы Эстемировой о маме-правозащитнице и детстве в военной и кадыровской Чечне — скоро в издательстве «Медузы» В 2025 году в британском издательстве John Murray вышла книга Ланы Эстемировой, дочери убитой в 2009 году правозащитницы, журналистки и сотрудницы «Мемориала» Натальи Эстемировой «Please Live: The Chechen Wars, My Mother and Me». Вот как о книге после выхода англоязычного издания говорила сама Лана: С самого начала мне было важно, чтобы эта книжка была не только для экспертов по Чечне или России, не только для журналистов или людей, которые знали мою маму. Я хотела, чтобы в центре повествования были не война, не Кадыров, не убийство, а история матери-одиночки и ее дочери. Таких семей немало, и история женщины, которая отдается работе, а ребенок растит себя сам, будет понятна очень многим. У окружающих был вопрос, почему она это делала, почему не уехала в другой регион России или за границу, почему она всю себя отдала этой работе, которая в конце концов привела ее к гибели. Я пыталась не искать ответ на этот вопрос, а просто показать, какой была наша жизнь. Чтобы читатель сам смог прийти к ответу, стоило ли оно этого. ✽ ✽ ✽ Спустя год «Пожалуйста, живи. Чеченские войны, мама и я» наконец выходит и на русском языке — в издательстве «Медузы» (чему мы страшно рады!). Она уже в типографии и скоро на нее откроется предзаказ. Как всегда, книга будет доступна в бумажном и электронном виде, следите за нашими обновлениями.

title: Telegram Post from meduzalive

description: Top Gear вернется в эфир спустя четыре года, сообщила The Sun По данным британского издания, BBC Studios начала работу над перезапуском автомобильного шоу Top Gear. По словам источника газеты, руководство «Би-би-си» стремится возродить Top Gear, потому что «так и не смогло заполнить пустоту после его ухода, и многие зрители скучают по автомобильному шоу». Бывших ведущих шоу Пэдди Макгиннеса, Криса Харриса и Фредди Флинтоффа, по информации The Sun, заменит новая команда. Поиск ведущих уже идет. Top Gear выходила с 1977 года, а одним из главных ведущих шоу был Джереми Кларксон, который вел его вместе с Ричардом Хаммондом и Джеймсом Мэем с 2002 до 2015 год. После конфликта с продюсером Кларксон был уволен, Хаммонд и Мэй ушли вслед за ним и стали вести шоу The Grand Tour. В декабре 2022 года «Би-би-си» приостановила выпуск Top Gear после того, как один из ведущих шоу, бывший капитан сборной Англии по крикету Эндрю «Фредди» Флинтофф, получил тяжелую травму на испытательном треке в Суррее. @meduzalive

title: Telegram Post from tproger
ad_reason: Brand mention detected without enough advertising evidence.
description: Posit выпустили ggsql — грамматику графики прямо в SQL Надоело экспортировать таблицу → открывать Python → подключать matplotlib только ради одного графика? Posit (бывшая RStudio, поддерживают ggplot2 и tidyverse) выпустили альфа-версию ggsql — расширения SQL, где график описывается прямо в запросе. Добавляете к SELECT ключевые слова VISUALIZE и DRAW — и таблица сразу превращается в scatter, гистограмму или boxplot. Никакого промежуточного датафрейма, никакого переключения инструментов. В основе — «грамматика графики»: концепция, где любой график собирается из модульных частей (данные, оси, геометрия, шкалы). Именно на ней построен ggplot2, ставший стандартом в R. Теперь тот же подход — в SQL. В планах: Rust-рендерер, интерактивность и поддержка LSP (автодополнение в IDE). Как это работает — читайте на Tproger — и, кажется, скоро аналитику незачем будет выходить из SQL-клиента вообще. @tproger Читайте также в VK , Max и Дзен

description: jj — CLI поверх Git, где конфликты решаются автоматически, а любую операцию можно откатить Представьте Git без staging area, где rebase не останавливается на конфликтах, а любую ошибку можно отменить одной командой. Это не фантазия — это jj, CLI для Jujutsu, который работает поверх вашего .git/ и не требует от коллег ничего менять. Ключевые отличия: рабочая копия — это коммит (любое изменение файла формирует новую версию с тем же change ID). Конфликты хранятся в истории как часть коммита, а не как маркеры в файле — rebase продолжается, потомки перестраиваются автоматически. И главное: jj undo откатывает любую операцию. Потерять работу в jj сложнее, чем в Git. Стив Клабник (автор «The Rust Programming Language») написал учебник по jj — с объяснением концепций и тем, чем они отличаются от Git. @tproger Читайте также в VK , Max и Дзен

description: GitHub запустил нативные Stacked PRs — альтернатива Graphite Если ваши пулл-реквесты разрастаются до 2000 строк и ревьюеры тонут в диффе — у GitHub теперь есть родной способ разбить большую задачу на стэк маленьких PR. Stacked PRs пока в Private Preview, но CLI-клиент gh stack уже опубликован (правда, работает только если вас пустили в preview). Stacked PR — это цепочка пулл-реквестов, где каждый нацелен не на main, а на ветку PR ниже. Вместо одного огромного реквеста с пятью разными по смыслу изменениями вы делаете пять маленьких, которые читаются независимо и строятся один на другом. Раньше стэки делали сторонними инструментами: облачный Graphite, консольные ghstack от Meta или spr. Все они симулировали стэки поверх обычного GitHub и ломались на ребейзах, merge queue и branch protection. Нативная реализация разобрали на Tproger — теперь можно не объяснять джунам, почему у команды три CLI для одного пулл-реквеста. @tproger Читайте также в VK , Max и Дзен

description: В терминале всего 33 Ctrl-шортката — и это не случайность Замечали, что Ctrl-1 ничего не делает, а Ctrl-A работает? ASCII физически даёт только 33 control-кода — остальные комбинации терминал либо игнорирует, либо превращает в ANSI escape-последовательность (управляющие символы для цвета и позиционирования курсора). Julia Evans разобрала всю таблицу. Ctrl-M и Enter — буквально один байт (код 13), исторически так сложилось. Ctrl-коды обрабатываются на трёх уровнях: одни забирает ОС, другие — bash или readline (библиотека редактирования командной строки), третьи добираются до приложения. Отсюда конфликты: Ctrl-S «зависает» терминал из-за поведения ОС, унаследованного из эпохи аппаратного управления потоком данных. Большинство нажимали эти комбинации годами, не задаваясь вопросом «почему». Теперь есть разбор целиком на Tproger . @tproger Читайте также в VK , Max и Дзен

title: Why Companies Are 'Debranding'
ad_reason: Consistent brand evidence detected without official disclosure.
transcript: In recent years, some of the world's biggest companies have discarded depth and detail  to deep-round.  Burger King lost weight, rolling stone, founder, cleaner-edge, VW shared its depth and shadow,  as did Keer, Pfizer, Nistan, Jurex, Intel, Toyota, and a host of other major brands.  Even the much-able hero Julius Pringle had a flattening make under, with shaved head,  made moustache, dilated eyes, and new, pre-sprung eyebrows.  But what prompted this landslide of logo deep-rounding?  Several interlocking forces are at work.  The most immediate of which is the pressure of mobile-first design.  Clients used to ask, can you make the logo bigger?  Now the trick is to shrink an entire identity into a tiny digital box.  Such pixel pressure usually means returning to the 2D look of old.  Of course, this 2D look was more or less the norm until computers took over and  design inflation spiraled out of control.  Anyone who is overfilted in Instagram sunset knows the seductive lure of visual access,  and it's a seduction to which the pros are not immune.  The ability to round corners, dropshadows, customize gradients, and create complex lighting  effects can easily overpower the creative brief, and often does.  So many recently brands represent a return to sobriety after a spasm of software-abetted  intoxication.  There was also the question of maturity.  Many of our biggest brands were born in a spirit of playful innocence that burst  forth from their early branding, take four square, Airbnb, GoDaddy, Google, as these companies  grew and the stakes rose, so their logos were obliged to mature.  From cartoonish to corporate, flamboyance of flat, wacky to bland, illustrating the  power of the branding to professionalise.  And then there is fashion.  Company graphics are as susceptible to trends as in the other design, as we see in the  stampede of fashion's little black dress logos, heralded by E. Sallarar in 2012, and followed  by Diane von Firstenberg, Calding Klein, Berlutie, Burberry, and Balmay.  The most intriguing element of the deep-rounded logo is its potential to become a portal.  One of the brothers didn't just strip back its theatrical golden gild, it unlocked a world  of possibility that increasingly vital to brands that spange genres showcase variety or offer  31 flavors.  For all these reasons, it seems likely that deep branding will be with us for a while, but  who knows when the pendulum will swing and the trend revert to detail, complexity, and  personality.  After all, once a critical mass is sand against the prevailing zig, the margin becomes  mainstream, the template flips, and the dance begins afresh.  I'm Ben Short, the branding and advertising columnist for Bloomberg opinion.

transcript: Good afternoon, welcome to the in a list, today we will be taking up 7 newspaper articles  from the Hindu and the Indian Express and discussing them.  The handout for the same will be available in the description of this video for your  kind periodial.  Please have a look at that.  The first article that will take up is related to the interstate water disputes as Rajasthan  is seeking a permanent seat on the Bhakada Bias Management Board saying that it also  has a share in the Ravi Bias waters.  The second article is related to the Indian monsoon as the monsoon is likely to reach  Kerala on May 26th as per the Indian meteorological department.  Additionally, we will take up 5 articles in the Pilim's point of section which will be helpful  for your Pilim's preparation.  The first article is related to interstate water disputes.  As Rajasthan seeks to have a permanent seat on the Bhakada Bias Management Board.  As it says that it also has a share in the Ravi Bias waters and it holds about 52% share  in the Ravi Bias waters.  So we will cover the topic related to interstate water disputes by taking up 3 case studies,  the ones related to the sharing of the waters of Madhaya River, Kaviri River and the southern  Yumna Linkanal which have been in the news in the past.  Will cover the challenges in the resolution of the interstate water disputes specifically  focusing upon the governance-related challenges and also a few suggestions in this regard.  This is important in GS2 under the topic dispute-redressal mechanisms and institutions  and also in GS1 in geography and in GS3 environment under the topic Water.  First let us start with the 3 case studies.  The first case study that will take up is related to the dispute in the sharing of the Madhaya  River Water.  The Madhaya River has the Bias in in 3 states, Karnatka, Goa and Maharashtra.  The dispute is chiefly between two states, Karnatka and Goa because Karnatka wishes  to divert some water of the Madhaya River to the Mal Prabhabbasin through the Kalsabandurin  Alla Project because Karnatka wishes to fulfill the drinking water needs of a few districts  in Karnatka that is Bellgavi, Dharwad and Gadaq districts.  Also Bellgavi is the sugar bowl of Karnatka and the farmers there need more water.  Therefore it wishes to transfer some of the water of the Madhaya River to the Mal Prabhabbasin.  Now the Madhaya River originates in the beam god wildlife sanctuary in Karnatka and flows through  Maharashtra and then through Goa and then drains into the Arabian Sea near Panchi.  Now it has several tributaries including Rogaro, Kushavati, Nanurim, Nanush, Valvota, Mapusa, Kalsanalah  etc.  And the basin area of Madhaya River is mainly in Goa, 78% in Goa, 18% in Karnatka and a small  portion about 4% in Maharashtra as well.  Now the dispute is because of the project of Karnatka that is Kalsanalah Bandurin project.  Now for the resolution of this dispute in 2010 the centre set up the tribunal, the Madhaya  Water Disputes Tribunal, why the Tribunal?  Because the Interstate Water Disputes in India they are not to be resolved through the Judiciary.  Rather through Article 2602 the Parliament is empowered to set up the tribunals for the  Resolution of the Interstate Water Disputes and therefore through the Interstate Water Disputes  Act of 1956 specifically section 4 of the Interstate Water Disputes Act 1956.  The centre government set up tribunals for the Resolution of the Interstate Water Disputes,  therefore the Madhaya Water Disputes Tribunal was set up.  Now the Tribunal gave its verdict in 2018, observing that all the three states Karnatka  Goa and Maharashtra have a share in the river water of the Madhaya River.  But Goa had certain concerns, for example that if more water is diverted from the Madhaya  River to the Malpura basin then the navigation in Mandovi in Goa may be affected, although  the Tribunal observed that Karnatka has a share of about 13 TMC feet, Goa will have the  access to over 24 TMC feet and Maharashtra about 1.3 TMC feet.  Now coming to the second case study, the dispute related to the sharing of the Kaviri river water.  The history of this dispute goes long back, this also has a basin in three states and one union  territory. The Kaviri river basin lies in the states of Tamil Nadu, Karnatka and Kerala  and the union territory of Pudujiri. This has a long history in 1892 onwards,  there was a dispute between the Presidency of Madhara's and the Presidency State of Mysore  regarding the sharing of the Kaviri river water. And in 1924, the Presidency of Madhara's  and the Presidency State of Mysore arrived upon an agreement regarding the sharing of the Kaviri river water.  This agreement was for a period of 50 years. Now this agreement expired in 1974.  Therefore, after 1974, Karnatka started diverting the water of Kaviri towards the filling of  four reservoirs in Karnatka. Naturally, this led to a grievance in Tamil Nadu which started facing  water scarcity. Therefore, in 1990, the Kaviri water dispute tribunal was constituted again  because of the interstate water disputes at 1956 and through the article 262.  Therefore, the Kaviri water dispute tribunal after a long period after 17 years finally gave  its award in 2007, observing that the water shares of both the states are fixed and they will  vary depending upon the different periods of the year. And there will also be the establishment  of a monitoring authority to ensure that the tribunal award is implemented, but the tribunal  did not give any detailed formula. If there is a scarcity of water, if there is a deficiency  in the rainfall itself, rather it observed that when there are insufficient rains, then the  proportion of the water of Kaviri which will be available for both Karnatka and Tamil Nadu  will be reduced in a proportionate manner. In 2013, again after a gap of six years  government finally notified the order, but subsequently Karnatka observed that it is unable to  implement the tribunal award. This led to a grievance in Tamil Nadu which approached the  Supreme Court finally. And the Supreme Court in 2018 finally gave the judgment regarding the  Kaviri revert water dispute, observing that Kaviri is a national asset and it broadly upheld  the water sharing arrangements which were finalized by the Kaviri water dispute tribunal. And  it observed that there can be the establishment of a Kaviri management scheme and also a Kaviri  water management authority as well. Coming to the third case study, it is related to the  Satsulu Yomna link in all. In 1955, the Ravi and Biaz river water sur measured, the Ravi and  Biaz river waters were calculated and divided between the riparian states. But meanwhile in 1966,  the Punjab reorganization act was enacted. Punjab and Haryana became separate states.  Therefore, the Satsulu Yomna link in all was planned. As per the plan, about 120 kilometers  of the Kerala was to lie in Punjab and that was to be constructed by Punjab and about 90  kilometers in Haryana that was to be constructed by Haryana. By 1980, Haryana completed its portion  of the Satsulu Yomna link in all. But in Punjab, there were protests by the Shiromani Akali Dal,  which led to the failure of Punjab in the completion of the Satsulu Yomna link in all.  Meanwhile, simultaneously, in 1985, there was insurgency in Punjab and there was an accord  with the Shiromani Akali Dal. The Prime Minister's accord with the Shiromani Akali Dal and the subsequent  establishment of the iraditrabnal to determine the sharing of the river water between Haryana and Punjab.  But there was also militancy in Punjab, which led to the killing of even the superintendent in  junior and the chief engineer, which were involved in the construction of the Satsulu Yomna link  in Punjab, which led to a long delay and finally, Haryana approached Supreme Court.  And in 2004, Supreme Court gave direction to Punjab to start the work on the Satsulu Yomna link  in all. But Punjab came off with an act, the Punjab termination of agreements act through which it  abrogated all its promises with respect to the sharing of the river waters with the other states.  The President of India referred it to the Supreme Court to examine its legality, its validity,  and in 2016, the Supreme Court observed that the Punjab termination of agreements act is invalid,  it is illegal and it will have to abide by the agreements. In 2020, the Supreme Court even  observed that the chief ministers of the two states Haryana and Punjab, they should negotiate  and the centric and mediate to resolve the issue of the sharing of the river water sender construction  of the Satsulu Yomna link in all. Now article 262, bars the jurisdiction of the court in the  resolution of the interstate water disputes. Therefore, through the interstate water disputes  at 1956, the centric sets up the tribunals and the tribunals gave the awards, the  adjudication of the interstate water disputes is done by the tribunals. Now, temporary tribunals  were being established and they were dissolved after they gave the award, but their award had the  force of a Supreme Court decree and they were binding upon the states which approached the tribunal  and when it gave the award, it had to be implemented. But there were challenges in the  mechanism of the interstate water disputes resolution. The governance challenges included the  adversarial litigations. That is, whichever state was agreed from the award given by the tribunal,  it approached the Supreme Court. It approached the Supreme Court utilizing the root of article 136  that is filing a specially petition because otherwise, the jurisdiction of the court's  barred because of the virtue of the article 262. Now, it led to chronic delays in the final  adjudication, in the finality of the implementation of the dispute resolution awards.  Also, there were no avenues to address the grievances of the states after the tribunal was  dissolved. That is, earlier the tribunals used to be temporary tribunals, which got dissolved  after they announced the award. Additionally, it even led to a kind of a constitutional crisis.  By pitching the legislature against the judiciary, for example, Karnatka expressed its  inability in the implementation of the Kaviri River Water Dispute Tribunal award.  And also, there was an institutional vacuum regarding its implementation because once the  tribunal announced the award, it was dissolved. After that, there was a vacuum regarding its implementation,  although the tribunal usually suggested for the creation of a monitoring authority to ensure  that there is the implementation of its award. And the decisions they are usually taken based  upon the vote banks politics. And also, the deficit year's allocation was usually not  deemed into deeply by the tribunals. That is, what would happen? What would be the criteria for  the allocation of the waters for the sharing of the waters? In case there are insufficient  rains in a year, in case there are deficit years. And there was an absence of transparent  information gathering and sharing as well. Now, what are the solutions that we can give for  the resolution of the interstate river water disputes? First, we need to take up a multi-pronged approach.  That we should put in place the legal measures, plus institutional measures, plus the technological  aspect also needs to be taken into account. Because only the enactment of a law may not suffice,  beneath the institutions to back it up to ensure that there are enforcement mechanisms.  And the institutions they should also take into account the technological aspect. For  example, the tribunal itself, before announcing the award, should take into account the hydrological  data, collect the basic level data, find out the socio-economic conditions in the states,  find out the needs and the capacities of the states then come up with the award.  The interstate river water disputes amendment bill 2019 also proposed good solutions.  For example, first, the dispute should be referred to a dispute resolution committee.  The dispute resolution committee should try to resolve it within a period of one year,  which can be extended by another six months. If the dispute cannot be resolved at the level of  a dispute resolution committee, then it can be referred to a permanent tribunal. And the permanent  travel will resolve it within a period of two years, give the award within two years, which can be  extended by another one year. Also, there should be the establishment of an expert agency  for the collection of data that is the basic level data should be collected. For example,  the rainwater data, irrigation-related data, surface water availability data, so that the  tribunal award that is based upon data, facts and scientific considerations, rather than  the political influence. Also, we can utilize the interstate council as a platform to ensure that  the states arrive at a solution, emicably through discussions and the distribution of the  waters between the states. It should not be based upon equality, rather it should be based upon  equity. For that, we can refer to the Helsinki rules and the campion rules that were also  referred to by the Supreme Court while giving the judgment in the Kaviri river water dispute case.  And the state is not the owner of the water. Rather, the state functions like a public  electricity, because the water is a resource that is shared by all, and we can also follow the approach  of 4R that is reduce recycle, reuse, and recover water to ensure that there is sustainability  in the usage of water as well in the longeren. There is a main practice question also,  discuss the major challenges associated with interstate water disputes in India,  what are the constitutional and institutional mechanisms available for their resolution?  Suggest measures to ensure cooperative and sustainable water governance in India,  please try to write it. The next article is related to the Indian monsoon. As the monsoon is expected  to reach Kerala by May 26th as per the observations of the Indian meteorological department.  So, we will discuss the factors influencing the Indian monsoon, the impact of the climate change  upon the Indian monsoon and the role of the Indian meteorological department. This is important  in GS1 under the topic important geophysical phenomena. First, let us observe a few basics regarding  the Indian monsoon. The monsoon can be termed as a system of seasonal winds, seasonal winds which  reverse their direction depending upon the change in the seasons. For example, in the summer months  from June to September, they are termed as the south-west monsoon, because they are coming from  the ocean towards the land. From the Indian Ocean towards the Indian Peninsula, coming from the  south-west direction, therefore termed as the south-west monsoon. Similarly, after the change in the  seasons in the October number months, they are termed as a retreating monsoon. As they are coming  from the north-east direction, therefore termed as the northeast monsoon, going back from the  land towards the ocean, regarding the timeline, in the normal conditions, they approach the  coast of Kerala around first of June and regarding the withdrawal, they leave the western  coast of India around 10th 15th October. So, they can be viewed as large convection cells,  therefore causing rainfall over India. Now, what are the factors which influence the Indian monsoon?  The primary factor is the differential heating of land and sea. That the land and water,  they heat up at different rates. Why? Because they have different specific heat capacities.  We can measure the heat in terms of Q is equal to MCP delta T. You don't need to remember the  question. It's for your understanding only. If the specific heat capacity is higher,  then the rise in temperature will be lower for the same amount of heat. Therefore, the rise in  temperature of the oceans during the summer months is lower. As compared to the rise in temperature  of the land, the land heats up quickly during the summer months, which causes a pressure gradient.  A low pressure situation over the land. Therefore, the heating of the Tibetan plateau, for example,  it makes sure that the air is coming from the ocean towards the land, which causes the  seasonal system of the winds causing the south-west monsoon over India. This is further influenced  by the shifting of the inter-tropical convergent zone. The inter-tropical convergent zone is a narrow  zone, which if the sun is on the equator, it lies upon the equator, where the trade winds are  converging. But during the summer months, the inter-tropical converges zone itself moves. For example,  during the summer months, the inter-tropical converges zone shifts northward. From the equator,  it shifts northward. You can observe this in the diagram also. Whereas, in the winter months,  it comes back and shifts downwards towards the south direction. And therefore, this becomes a  zone of strong convection. A zone of strong convection, where there is a low pressure,  because of the low pressure, the air is rising. When the air is rising, there is the formation  of the clouds, the formation of the clouds and causing the rainfall also. And in the early  zone, therefore, the inter-tropical convergent zone has shifted northward. And it has shifted towards  the northern India. Therefore, attracting the south-west monsoon from the ocean towards the  land. Similarly, during the October month, the inter-tropical convergent zone has further shifted  back south then. And therefore, attracting those winds down south and therefore, the same winds  they become the north-east monsoon. And when the monsoon winds, they encounter an altitude also,  then they are forced to rise. When they are rising, it leads to the cooling of the winds,  therefore, condensation and therefore, the causing of the rainfall, which causes the rainfall  in the western-guards region, as well as near the Himalayas. The further factors influencing the Indian  monsoon are the prevalence of El Nino, La Nina, like conditions. The El Nino and La Nina,  they are climatic events influenced by the temperatures in the Pacific Ocean.  When there are normal conditions, that is, there is no El Nino, there is the availability of  warm water on the surface near the east coast of Australia, which becomes a downwelling zone.  And therefore, the air over here is rising. When the air is rising, there is the formation of the  clouds, there is a convecting curve. And therefore, there is rainfall over the east coast of Australia.  This air travels east towards the east Pacific Ocean. And therefore, it subsides near the  south coast of America near the Peru, where there is upwelling zone and also the availability of  cold current. Therefore, this air then finally travels back, along the surface of the ocean  back towards the east coast of Australia. Simultaneously, inside the water also, the current  is formed. And there becomes a downwelling zone near the east coast of Australia and the upwelling  zone near Peru. When the conditions become El Nino like conditions, then this convecting  conditions, they shift eastward. Therefore, near the east coast of Australia, now rather,  there is the availability of cold water. And therefore, this becomes a zone of subsidence,  which causes lower rainfall over India. Therefore, when the El Nino conditions exist,  there is lower rainfall during the monsoon season over India, whereas when the La Nina conditions  La Nina simply strengthens the walker circulation, it leads to huge rainfall over India.  Another factor is the Indian Ocean dipole. The Indian Ocean dipole is due to the  temperature gradient within the Indian Ocean itself. When there is negative Indian Ocean dipole,  then the eastern Indian Ocean is warmer than normal, which leads to the air rising near the  eastern ocean. Indian Ocean and it travels towards the west Indian Ocean and it subsides over  the east coast of Africa. This again causes lower rainfall over India. The positive  Indian dipole is beneficial for more rainfall over India. Another factor influencing the Indian  monsoon is the Medangulian oscillation. The Medangulian oscillation has an eastward movement.  It has a cycle of 30 to 60 days. That is, it has two phases. One phase is that of strong  convection, another phase is that of separation. When there is strong convection phase at a place,  then there is a strong updraft of the air. The air moves up and when the air is moving up,  it leads to the formation of the clouds when it is over the ocean. Therefore, when over the western  Indian Ocean, there is strong convective phase of the Medangulian oscillation. This air will then  come towards the Indian Peninsula, which will cause more rainfall over the Indian Peninsula. Whereas  if there is a suppression phase over the western Indian Ocean, the suppression phase of the  Medangulian oscillation, this will decrease the rainfall over peninsolar India. Similarly,  another factor is the sea surface temperature. Однако вряд ли можно сказать, что их присутствие сильно на что-то повлияло. Более того, после двух дней, которые Трамп провел в Пекине, в принципе мало понятно, зачем именно он приезжал. А вот Си Цзиньпин четко знал, какого результата хочет добиться от этой встречи. Именно так считает китаист и директор Берлинского центра Карнеги по изучению России и Евразии Александр Габуев. В тексте для проекта Carnegie Politika он анализирует встречу двух лидеров, а также рассказывает, чего дальше ожидать от отношений Китая и США. «Медуза» публикует этот текст целиком. description: В Екатеринбурге у дома, поврежденного беспилотниками, появилось граффити с надписью «Game over» Новый стрит-арт появился рядом с жилым комплексом «Тринити», в который в конце апреля попал украинский беспилотник. На граффити изображена девочка с пультом управления дрона в VR-очках и надписью «Game over». Предполагается, что что это работа арт-группы «Злые». 25 апреля украинские беспилотники впервые атаковали Екатеринбург. В результате был поврежден многоэтажный жилой дом комплекса «Тринити».
